testBaseline.note: Ruff errors are pre-existing in src/server/ai/ollama.py and tests/test_remote_ollama.py — not sprint scope

stories.title: Create FastAPI project scaffold and config in src/server/
stories.intent: Stand up the server app skeleton so all subsequent crawl stories have a running FastAPI process to build on. Replaces the placeholder main.py and app.py created during the reorg.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
src/server/main.py creates FastAPI app via lifespan handler, runnable with uvicorn src.server.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
src/server/config.py uses Pydantic Settings reading from .env with all variables from spec §Configuration Variables (DATABASE_URL required, all others with defaults)
requirements-server.txt lists: fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, aiomysql, pydantic-settings, python-multipart, httpx, alembic, pytest, pytest-asyncio
.env.example updated with all server config variables and descriptions
verification: python -c "from src.server.config import Settings; s = Settings(_env_file='.env.example'); print(s.PORT)"
python -c "from src.server.api.app import createApp; app = createApp(); print(app.title)"
invariants: No changes to src/pi/ or src/common/
stopConditions: If Pydantic Settings cannot read DATABASE_URL from .env, STOP and file BL-

feedback.grounding: PORT default 8000, LOG_LEVEL default INFO per spec §1.1. DATABASE_URL required, all 13 other fields have defaults. Pydantic Settings reads .env with env_file_encoding utf-8.
feedback.notes: Files created by prior Agent2 session (unstaged). This session fixed 7 ruff lint errors (unused imports, Optional→X|None, import sorting) and verified all ACs. Server deps (fastapi, pydantic-settings) not installed in dev venv — import verification deferred to deploy.

stories.title: Create MariaDB schema and async connection in src/server/db/
stories.intent: Define all server-side database tables (synced + server-only + analytics) so the data loader and analytics stories have a schema to work against.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change

src/server/db/models.py defines SQLAlchemy models for all 11 tables from spec §1.2: realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions (synced), sync_history, analysis_history, devices (server-only), drive_summary, drive_statistics, trend_snapshots, anomaly_log (analytics)
Every synced table has id (PK autoincrement), source_id (INT NOT NULL), source_device (VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL), UNIQUE(source_device, source_id)
src/server/db/connection.py provides async SQLAlchemy session factory using aiomysql
deploy/setup-mariadb.sh creates obd2db and obd2db_test databases, grants privileges to user obd2 from 10.27.27.%
verification: python -c "from src.server.db.models import RealtimeData, DriveSummary; print('Models imported')"
stopConditions: If aiomysql cannot connect to MariaDB at localhost, STOP — server DB may not be running

feedback.grounding: 15 tables total: 8 synced (realtime_data, statistics, profiles, vehicle_info, ai_recommendations, connection_log, alert_log, calibration_sessions) with id/source_id/source_device/UNIQUE pattern, 3 server-only (sync_history, analysis_history, devices), 4 analytics (drive_summary, drive_statistics, trend_snapshots, anomaly_log). Database name obd2db, test db obd2db_test, user obd2@10.27.27.% per spec §1.2.
feedback.notes: Pi schema columns mirrored from database_schema.py. vehicle_info PK changed from VIN (Pi TEXT PK) to autoincrement INT with source_id pattern. aiomysql not installed on Windows dev — engine creation test skipped. 65 tests pass, 1 skip. Setup script includes localhost access in addition to subnet.

stories.title: Create systemd service file and install script for server
stories.intent: Enable the server to run as a persistent systemd service on Chi-Srv-01, auto-restarting on failure.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
deploy/obd2-server.service is Type=simple, Restart=on-failure, RestartSec=10, After=network.target mysql.service, ExecStart runs uvicorn on port 8000
deploy/install-server.sh copies service file, runs daemon-reload, enable, start, and prints status

feedback.grounding: RestartSec=10 per spec §1.4. Port 8000 per spec §1.1. After=network.target mysql.service. Type=simple, Restart=on-failure. Install script: daemon-reload, enable, start, status. Default user mcornelison, path /home/mcornelison/Projects/OBD2v2.
feedback.notes: Modeled after existing Pi service (eclipse-obd.service) and install script (install-service.sh). Added --port flag and uvicorn/main.py/env validation. Shell scripts not lintable by ruff (Python only). Pi service unchanged (invariant verified).

stories.title: Implement GET /health endpoint with component status
stories.intent: Give the CIO a single URL to check whether the server, database, and Ollama are healthy. First real route in the FastAPI app.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
GET /api/v1/health returns JSON with status (healthy/degraded/unhealthy), version, components (api/mysql/ollama), lastSync, lastAnalysis, driveCount, uptime
status=healthy when mysql=up AND ollama=up; degraded when mysql=up but ollama issues; unhealthy when mysql=down
No auth required on /health

feedback.grounding: Response envelope and status matrix per spec §1.3: healthy iff mysql=up AND ollama=up; degraded when mysql=up, ollama issues; unhealthy when mysql=down. No auth on /health per §2.1. Uptime formatted 'Xd Yh Zm' per spec example '2d 4h 30m'.
feedback.notes: 21 new tests added (8 pure helpers + 13 endpoint/matrix/no-auth/component-check via TestClient). All pass with fastapi+httpx+pydantic-settings+sqlalchemy installed locally; module-level pytest.importorskip('fastapi') + ('httpx') lets the suite skip cleanly on machines without server deps. Router wired under /api/v1 via createApp; main.py lifespan now creates async engine and sets app.state.startTime / app.state.engine, disposing engine on shutdown. Ruff clean on all touched files. Full regression: 1592 passed, 2 pre-existing timing-flaky simulator e2e tests failed (test_driveDetectionWorks, test_databaseContainsExpectedRecords — unrelated to server work).

stories.title: Create seed_scenarios.py to export simulator data to portable SQLite
stories.intent: Generate fake drive data from the existing Pi simulator and export it to standalone SQLite files that the server data loader can consume. This is the crawl phase's data source — no Pi hardware needed.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
scripts/seed_scenarios.py --scenario city_driving --output data/sim_city.db creates a valid SQLite file with realtime_data, connection_log, and statistics tables
scripts/seed_scenarios.py --all --output data/sim_all.db runs all 4 scenarios into one database with multiple drive sessions
Output SQLite file uses the same schema as Pi's local database (realtime_data columns match)
Time-scaled execution: 17-minute scenario runs in under 10 seconds
verification: python scripts/seed_scenarios.py --scenario city_driving --output data/test_seed.db && python -c "import sqlite3; c=sqlite3.connect('data/test_seed.db'); print(c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM realtime_data').fetchone())"
invariants: No modifications to src/pi/obd/simulator/ source files
stopConditions: If existing simulator classes cannot be imported outside of --simulate mode, STOP and file BL-

feedback.grounding: 4 scenarios confirmed: city_driving (141s sim, 2115 rows), highway_cruise, cold_start, full_cycle. Wall clock 0.28s for city_driving. Pi schema reused from database_schema.py. 21 tests passing.
feedback.notes: Required adding src/ to sys.path in script because pi.obd.__init__.py uses `from pi.display import ...` which needs src/ on path. Tests pass because conftest.py already adds src/.

stories.title: Create load_data.py to import SQLite exports into MariaDB
stories.intent: Bridge between the seed script output and the MariaDB analytics schema. This is the crawl-phase data pipeline — CIO runs it manually over SSH after generating simulator data.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
scripts/load_data.py --db-file data/sim_city.db --device-id sim-eclipse-gst reads all Pi-schema tables from SQLite and bulk-inserts into MariaDB
Uses INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with upsert key (source_device, source_id)
After loading: detects drives from connection_log (drive_start/drive_end pairs), creates drive_summary rows
Prints summary: rows loaded per table, drives detected, elapsed time
invariants: Idempotent — running twice on same data produces same MariaDB state

feedback.grounding: Upsert keyed on (source_device, source_id) matches server spec §1.2. Pi SQLite rowid used as source_id (works for INTEGER PK tables and TEXT PK tables like profiles/vehicle_info). Profile PK 'id' (TEXT) renamed to server's source_profile_id column. Dialect-aware upsert: MySQL ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE / SQLite ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Drive detection pairs drive_start with next drive_end (second start replaces unmatched first — false-start semantics). DriveSummary idempotency via check-then-insert on (device_id, start_time).
feedback.notes: 21 tests pass (5 detectDrivePairs pure + 11 end-to-end via SQLite server stand-in + 4 CLI + 1 summary). Tests use SQLite as MariaDB stand-in (SQLAlchemy models port cleanly — schema verified via Base.metadata.create_all on sqlite). aiomysql not installed on Windows dev so MariaDB path exercised only via ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE SQL generation (not end-to-end). E2E verified with seed_scenarios.py city_driving → 2115 realtime + 1 drive detected; re-run produced same counts (idempotency invariant holds). Full regression 1597 passed, +21 from new tests. Ruff clean on both files.

stories.title: Implement basic analytics engine with per-drive statistics
stories.intent: First analytics layer: compute per-drive statistics and flag parameters that deviate from historical norms. This gives the CIO actionable data on individual drives.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
basic.py computeDriveStatistics(driveId) computes per-parameter: min, max, avg, std_dev, outlier_min (mean - 2*std), outlier_max (mean + 2*std), sample_count; stores results in drive_statistics table
basic.py compareDriveToHistory(driveId) flags parameters where current drive deviates > 2 standard deviations from historical aggregates; returns status per parameter: NORMAL, WATCH, INVESTIGATE
types.py defines dataclasses for DriveStatistics, ParameterComparison, ComparisonStatus
Tests seed known data and assert exact statistic values
invariants: Analytics code has zero imports from src.pi.*

stories.title: Implement advanced analytics: trends, correlations, anomaly detection
stories.intent: Second analytics layer: detect multi-drive trends, cross-parameter correlations, and anomalies. This turns raw per-drive stats into tuning-relevant signals the CIO can act on.
stories.pmSignOff: Marcus — L-sized, most complex crawl story. Split if implementation exceeds 1000 lines diff.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change

computeTrends(parameterName, windowSize=10) computes rolling averages of peak and avg across last N drives; detects direction (rising/falling/stable) via linear regression slope significance; flags >5% drift
computeCorrelations() calculates Pearson r between known tuning-relevant pairs: IAT↔KnockCount, IAT↔STFT, RPM↔CoolantTemp, Boost↔AFR; flags |r| > 0.7 as significant
detectAnomalies(driveId) checks each parameter's statistics against historical envelope (mean ± 2*std across drives); writes anomaly_log with severity WATCH (2-3σ) or INVESTIGATE (>3σ)
Stores results in trend_snapshots and anomaly_log tables
Tests seed 12+ drives with known patterns and assert correct trend direction, correlation detection, and anomaly flagging
invariants: Analytics code has zero imports from src.pi.*
Correlation pairs are configurable, not hardcoded in function body
stopConditions: If diff exceeds 800 lines, STOP and propose splitting trend/correlation/anomaly into 3 sub-stories

stories.title: Create CLI report tool with drive and trend report formats
stories.intent: Make analytics output human-readable. CIO will SSH into Chi-Srv-01 and run this tool to inspect drive data and trends — this is the crawl phase's primary output interface.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
scripts/report.py --drive latest prints formatted drive report with parameter table (Min/Max/Avg/Std/Status) and historical comparison section, matching the format in spec §1.9
scripts/report.py --drive all prints summary table of all drives (date, duration, device, profile, row count)
scripts/report.py --trends prints trend report with direction arrows (↑↗→↘↓), delta over period, significance, and detected correlations, matching spec §1.9 format
scripts/report.py --trends --last 20 overrides default 10-drive trend window
invariants: Report format matches spec §1.9 examples (verified by test assertions on stdout capture)
